Engineering Technicians are Amazon’s front line when it comes to hands-on electrical and mechanical equipment troubleshooting. They will maintain, operate, and troubleshoot, Data Center Operation’s Mission Critical Facilities, which includes stand-by diesel generators and related fuel systems, as well as three phase electrical systems such as: switch gear, UPSs, PDUs, wet cell batteries and associated systems, CRAC, centrifugal chillers, cooling towers/water chemical system, air handlers and associated systems, pumps, & motors.

AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

NOTE: Amazon does not sponsor for immigration, including for H-1B, TN, and other non-immigrant visas, for this role.
NOTE: Lump sum stipend will be provided to eligible candidates who relocate for this position.


Key job responsibilities
- Daily building rounds to monitor and record information from electrical, mechanical, and fire/life safety equipment to ensure 24/7 reliability and availability
- Monitor Building Management Systems (BMS) and Electrical Power Management System (EPMS)
- Assist in troubleshooting of facility and rack-level events within internal Service Level Agreements (SLA)
- Perform rack installs and rack decommissioning within internal SLA
- Perform PM, troubleshooting, root cause analysis, and repair of electrical and mechanical equipment
- Technical writing to support change management program
- Monitor daily work requests and manage to resolution, and execute projects from conception to completion
- On-site support of contractors, sub-contractors and vendors, ensuring that all work performed is in accordance with established practices, procedures & local legislation
- Work schedule changes depending on specific site needs. Shifts can be up to 12 hours and may rotate on a predefined schedule. Some locations have on-call rotations.
- Role may support more than one location/site.

Physical Requirements:
- Willingness and demonstrated ability to work in the following physical conditions with reasonable accommodations:
- Regularly lift and/or move up to 39 pounds independently
- Participate in group lifts for 40+ pounds
- Maintain balance and perform construction tasks while on a ladder
- Regularly walk, use hands and fingers, handle or feel, reach with hands and arms, stoop, kneel, crouch or crawl, climb stairs, twist, bend, work above shoulders, grasp, carry, push and pull objects into position
- Work in an environment requiring provided ear protection

This role supports data centers in various operational stages. The position may be assigned to a facility still under development, which could require travel to established locations for training until your designated site becomes fully operational.

Essential Requirements:
- Ability to travel to or commute between data center locations as needed
- Willingness to temporarily work at alternative sites during training periods or until assigned facility is operational
- Travel frequency will vary based on business needs and operational status of assigned facility

Candidates must be able to travel (over 50 miles) or commute (less than 50 miles) to active data center sites as required by business needs

A day in the life
This role acts as Amazon’s front line when it comes to hands-on electrical and mechanical equipment preventative maintenance (PM) and troubleshooting, reporting to a site’s Data Center Facility Manager (FM). They will maintain, operate, and troubleshoot mission-critical data center facility equipment including stand-by diesel generators and related fuel systems, 3 phase electrical systems such as switchgear, UPS units, PDUs, and wet cell batteries and associated systems. Mechanical equipment includes CRAHU units, centrifugal chillers, cooling towers, water systems, air handlers and associated systems, pumps, and motors. Additional support equipment is included in the scope of the role which includes fire/life safety equipment, building automation systems, and general facilities equipment.

title: Line Cook
description:
Latitudes, at Sunset Key Cottages, is looking to add a Line Cook to our team. This is a great opportunity to be a part of an organization that believes our team members are our most important resource and therefore take great pride in selecting individuals that help us to achieve our company’s mission.

Benefits:

  • Health Insurance
  • Dental Insurance
  • Life Insurance
  • Vision Insurance
  • 401(k)
  • Free Associate Parking
  • Free Meal for every shift worked
  • 25% Discount in Resort Outlets
  • Hotel Discounts with OPL
  • Friends and Family Discount

Responsibilities:

  • Sets up the station in time for service according to restaurant guidelines
  • Prepares all food items as directed in a sanitary and timely manner
  • Follows recipes, portion controls, and presentation specifications as set by the chef or restaurant
  • Restocks all items as needed throughout the shift and is responsible for taking inventory after service
  • Cleans and maintains stations in practicing good safety, sanitation, organizational skills
  • Has understanding and knowledge to properly use and maintain all equipment in the station
  • Assists with the cleaning, sanitation, and organization of kitchen, walk-in coolers, and all storage areas
  • Performs additional responsibilities, although not detailed, as requested by the Chef, and Sous Chef at any time

Qualifications:

  • High School Diploma/GED a plus but not required
  • Previous experience a plus
  • A thorough and deep understanding of professional cooking including basic techniques and working food knowledge.
  • Understanding and knowledge of safety, sanitation and food handling procedures.
  • Previous prep or line cook experience.
  • Professional communications skills are required

The company also conducts post-offer employment verifications, motor vehicle, and criminal background checks.

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer. This company does not and will not discriminate in employment and personnel practices on the basis of race, sex, age, handicap, religion, national origin or any other basis prohibited by applicable law.

title: Loan Servicing Analyst
description:
Join a team where your expertise can improve client experiences and strengthen operational excellence. Bring your leadership, problem-solving, and training skills to help drive meaningful change.

Job summary

As a Loan Servicing Analyst in the Loan Servicing Operations team, you will lead training and project initiatives across multiple servicing teams while providing day-to-day support for queue execution, escalations, and service-level delivery. You will act as a subject matter expert and partner with stakeholders across operations, risk, legal, and audit to improve processes and outcomes. Required Job Description Format.docx

Job responsibilities

  • Partner with team members, frontline servicing managers, and key stakeholders (including risk, legal, and audit) to support servicing outcomes and issue resolution.
  • Serve as primary trainer for new and existing staff; create and maintain training materials for onboarding and refresher training, including updates due to procedure changes.
  • Monitor servicing work queues throughout the day; prioritize transactions by service levels, cutoffs, client impact, and business urgency to ensure timely completion.
  • Own procedure updates by gathering feedback from impacted managers and preparing content for publishing.
  • Identify and recommend process improvements that enhance efficiency, reduce unit cost, and improve client/service experience, including evaluating automation opportunities.
  • Act as a subject matter expert across multiple loan servicing functions and processes supported by the team.
  • Create test scripts and execute user acceptance testing across multiple applications and platforms.
  • Manage medium-to-complex research and escalations, including summarization and senior management notification when required.
  • Extract, create, and/or action routine and ad hoc business reports to support servicing and performance needs.
  • Provide production support during peak periods, staffing gaps, high-volume events, month-end activity, and other business-critical situations.
  • Process loan and line-of-credit transactions across multiple loan servicing systems and related tools (e.g., fundings, wire transfers, payments, rate and fee updates, payoff quotes, and manual invoicing).

Required qualifications, capabilities, and skills

  • Currently in an equivalent senior loan servicing specialist role within loan servicing operations.
  • Demonstrated strong productivity and quality performance metrics (e.g., consistently ranking in the top tier of the current team).
  • Ability to learn and work across multiple loan servicing systems and supporting tools.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Outlook).
  • Ability to adapt to internal tracking and monitoring tools.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills and strong interpersonal skills.
  • Strong customer service mindset and ability to partner effectively across teams.
  • Ability to multitask and prioritize effectively while working within tight service levels.
  • Strong organizational and time management skills.
  • High attention to detail and accuracy in completed work.
  • Meets internal mobility eligibility requirements, including tenure and satisfactory performance standards, and no active corrective action restrictions.

Preferred qualifications, capabilities, and skills

  • Experience leading onboarding, refresher training, and training content development for operational teams.
  • Experience owning or editing operating procedures for a servicing function.
  • Demonstrated experience supporting queue prioritization and service-level management in a high-volume environment.
  • Experience driving process improvement initiatives (including low-cost solutions and automation).
  • Experience performing user acceptance testing and writing test scripts.
  • Experience handling complex client or stakeholder escalations and preparing senior-ready summaries.
  • Experience supporting multiple servicing teams, workbaskets, or client segments concurrently.

title: Director of Housekeeping
description:
Pyramid Global Hospitality is a leading hospitality management company with a portfolio of more than 200 hotels and resorts and over 18,000 associates across the United States, Caribbean, and Europe. Our platform includes Benchmark Resorts & Hotels, a collection of distinctive independent properties; Axiom Hospitality, our European hotel management company; and PYRAMIDWORKS, which provides integrated workplace and facilities services. With corporate offices in Boston, The Woodlands, Texas, and London, Pyramid combines global scale with a hands-on approach focused on exceptional service, operational excellence, and long-term partnerships.


At Pyramid, our people are at the heart of everything we do. We are committed to creating an environment where associates are respected, supported, and empowered to grow their careers. Team members have the opportunity to collaborate directly with experienced leaders, take on meaningful stretch assignments, and gain exposure to a diverse portfolio of properties, brands, and markets. Whether you are beginning your hospitality career or bringing years of experience, you'll find opportunities to learn, develop, and make an impact.


Our commitment to associates, guests, owners, and the communities we serve has helped make Pyramid and our properties among the most recognized in hospitality. From being named among the best places to work by respected publications such as USA Today, The Boston Globe, and The Houston Chronicle to earning top travel accolades from Travel + Leisure, Condé Nast Traveler, Forbes Travel Guide, and U.S. News & World Report, these recognitions reflect the culture, service excellence, and commitment to people that define the Pyramid experience.

About our property:

At the La Quinta Resort and Club we pride ourselves on being the best employer in the area. What sets us apart? Our culture, full benefit plans, including 401k with a company match and our lucrative bonus programs, to name a few. We are looking for talented people who have a genuine passion for service. Our Core Values involve personal development, people, and a desire for wanting them to succeed. We believe each member of our team has a key part to play in our delivery of outstanding service and memorable experiences for each guest. We want a Resort that people want to come to, not through. Find out today what a career with Pyramid Hotel Group at the La Quinta Resort and Club can mean for you!

What you will have an opportunity to do:

We are seeking an experienced Director of Housekeeping to lead our Housekeeping and Laundry operations. This individual will be responsible for ensuring the highest standards of cleanliness, presentation, safety, and personalized service throughout the hotel. The Director of Housekeeping is a visible, hands-on leader who develops high-performing teams, maintains exceptional guestroom and public-area standards, and effectively manages labor, supplies, inventory, and departmental expenses. The successful candidate will demonstrate strong operational knowledge, attention to detail, financial awareness, and a genuine passion for creating memorable experiences for both guests and team members.

Essential Responsibilities

  • Provide strategic and daily leadership for Housekeeping, Public Areas, Turndown, and Laundry operations.

  • Recruit, train, coach, schedule, and develop managers, supervisors, room attendants, housepersons, public-area attendants, and laundry team members.

  • Establish clear expectations and hold leaders and team members accountable for quality, productivity, attendance, safety, and service.

  • Conduct daily briefings, departmental meetings, training sessions, and performance discussions.

  • Create a positive and inclusive work environment that promotes teamwork, recognition, professional development, and associate engagement.

  • Develop succession plans and identify opportunities for internal advancement.

  • Ensure departmental leaders are visible in the operation and actively support the team during high-volume periods.

Guestroom and Public-Area Standards

  • Ensure guestrooms, suites, public areas, offices, back-of-house spaces, and other assigned areas meet established cleanliness and presentation standards.

  • Conduct regular guestroom, public-area, linen-room, laundry, and storage-area inspections.

  • Establish consistent inspection procedures and document deficiencies, corrective actions, and follow-up.

  • Ensure rooms are cleaned, inspected, and released according to hotel occupancy demands and established check-in times.

  • Monitor VIP arrivals, special requests, room preferences, turndown service, and other personalized guest arrangements.

  • Partner with Front Office to manage room status, early arrivals, late departures, out-of-order rooms, and guest priorities.

  • Respond promptly and professionally to guest concerns involving cleanliness, room condition, lost items, or housekeeping service.

  • Identify recurring service or quality concerns and implement sustainable solutions.

Operational Management

  • Develop daily staffing plans based on occupancy, arrivals, departures, stayover service, group activity, and business levels.

  • Monitor room assignments, cleaning progress, inspection status, productivity, and room availability throughout the day.

  • Maintain accurate room-status communication between Housekeeping, Front Office, Engineering, and Reservations.

  • Establish and maintain cleaning schedules for guestrooms, public areas, deep cleaning, preventive maintenance, and special projects.

  • Coordinate room maintenance and repair needs with Engineering and verify completed work.

  • Oversee the proper handling, documentation, storage, and return of lost-and-found items.

  • Ensure all departmental equipment, carts, closets, pantries, and storage areas are clean, organized, secure, and properly maintained.

  • Participate in operational meetings, executive meetings, forecasting discussions, and hotel planning activities.

  • Serve as Manager on Duty when assigned.

Financial and Productivity Management

  • Prepare and manage the annual Housekeeping and Laundry operating budgets.

  • Monitor labor costs, payroll, overtime, productivity, supplies, linen replacement, uniforms, equipment, and contracted services.

  • Review financial statements, forecasts, and departmental performance reports.

  • Adjust staffing levels according to occupancy and business demands while maintaining service and quality standards.

  • Establish productivity expectations and evaluate performance without compromising safety, cleanliness, or the guest experience.

  • Identify cost-saving opportunities while protecting the hotel’s service standards and physical assets.

  • Review purchasing activity and ensure expenditures remain within approved budget guidelines.

  • Provide accurate information for monthly forecasts, variance explanations, and capital-planning needs.

Linen, Laundry, Supplies, and Inventory

  • Direct the purchasing, receiving, distribution, storage, and control of guest supplies, cleaning products, linen, terry, uniforms, and equipment.

  • Maintain appropriate par levels based on occupancy, business volume, laundry schedules, and operational needs.

  • Conduct and document regular inventories of linen, terry, uniforms, supplies, and equipment.

  • Monitor linen quality, loss, damage, discard rates, and replacement costs.

  • Ensure laundry operations meet established standards for quality, productivity, equipment care, and chemical usage.

  • Maintain secure and organized storage areas with appropriate inventory controls.

  • Manage relationships with vendors and outside laundry or cleaning contractors, when applicable.

Safety, Sanitation, and Compliance

  • Ensure compliance with hotel policies, brand standards, health regulations, safety requirements, and applicable employment laws.

  • Maintain current Safety Data Sheets and ensure cleaning products are labeled, stored, diluted, and used correctly.

  • Train team members on chemical safety, bloodborne pathogens, ergonomics, lifting procedures, personal protective equipment, and emergency response.

  • Regularly inspect equipment and work areas for hazards and immediately address unsafe conditions.

  • Document incidents, injuries, damages, guest concerns, and corrective actions accurately and promptly.

  • Partner with Human Resources and Security on investigations, workplace concerns, safety initiatives, and policy compliance.

  • Protect guest privacy, confidential information, room keys, master keys, and hotel assets.

Quality and Continuous Improvement

  • Analyze guest feedback, inspection scores, quality audits, service-recovery trends, and departmental performance data.

  • Develop action plans to address recurring cleanliness, maintenance, productivity, or service concerns.

  • Maintain readiness for brand inspections, health inspections, safety audits, and ownership reviews.

  • Introduce processes, technology, and training that improve communication, room readiness, productivity, and quality.

  • Collaborate with hotel leaders to support sustainability initiatives, including water conservation, waste reduction, linen-reuse programs, and responsible chemical usage.

Qualifications

  • High school diploma or equivalent required.

  • Bachelor’s degree in Hospitality Management, Business Administration, or a related field preferred.

  • Minimum of five years of progressive housekeeping leadership experience in a hotel, resort, or luxury hospitality environment.

  • Previous experience as a Director of Housekeeping, Executive Housekeeper, or Assistant Director of Housekeeping strongly preferred.

  • Experience managing a large housekeeping operation, multiple outlets or buildings, and high occupancy levels preferred.

  • Strong knowledge of guestroom cleaning procedures, public-area maintenance, laundry operations, chemical handling, and inventory control.

  • Demonstrated ability to manage labor, budgets, forecasts, productivity, and departmental expenses.

  • Strong leadership, coaching, communication, organization, and problem-solving skills.

  • Ability to analyze operational data and translate findings into practical action plans.

  • Proficiency with hotel property-management systems, housekeeping technology, scheduling systems, and Microsoft Office applications.

  • Ability to communicate effectively with guests, team members, vendors, and senior leadership.

  • Bilingual English and Spanish communication skills are preferred.

The ideal candidate will demonstrate:

  • Visible and hands-on leadership

  • Exceptional attention to detail

  • Accountability and sound judgment

  • Financial and operational awareness

  • Effective communication and collaboration

  • Coaching and talent-development skills

  • Professionalism and discretion

  • Composure in a fast-paced environment

  • A strong commitment to guest service

  • Respect for a diverse workforce

Physical and Scheduling Requirements

  • Ability to stand and walk for extended periods.

  • Ability to bend, reach, kneel, push, pull, and lift items typically associated with housekeeping operations.

  • Ability to inspect guestrooms, service areas, stairways, public spaces, and outdoor areas.

  • Ability to work safely around cleaning products, laundry equipment, and operational machinery.

  • Ability to work a flexible schedule based on business needs, including early mornings, evenings, weekends, holidays, and emergency situations.

Success in this position may be evaluated through:

  • Guest cleanliness and satisfaction scores

  • Room-readiness and inspection results

  • Brand and quality-assurance scores

  • Labor cost and productivity performance

  • Linen, supply, and operating-expense controls

  • Associate engagement, retention, and training completion

  • Workplace safety and incident trends

  • Preventive-maintenance and deep-cleaning completion

  • Guest concern response and resolution

  • Departmental budget performance

What are we looking for?

Compensation:

$115,000

-

$120,000

Pyramid Global Hospitality is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

Actual compensation packages are based on a wide array of factors unique to each candidate, including but not limited to skill set, years & depth of experience, certifications and specific office location. This may differ in other locations due to cost of labor considerations.

title: Power Line Technician
description:
You are a skilled Journeyperson or Apprentice Linesperson who supports the construction, installation, and maintenance of overhead and underground powerlines in Saskatchewan. You enjoy working at heights, operating specialized equipment, and ensuring that electrical and communication systems are installed safely and to industry standards.

If you are safety driven, physically capable, experienced in power line construction or eager to develop in the trade, join our Utilities team in Saskatchewan today!

Learn more about working at Ledcor: Watch here.

Essential Responsibilities:

  • Construct, install, inspect, and maintain overhead and underground power distribution systems

  • Operate a variety of hand and power tools and equipment including bucket trucks, backhoes, trenchers, skid steers, digger derrick trucks, all-terrain track machines, and trackhoes

  • Troubleshoot and repair distribution powerlines

  • Transport materials used for the installation of electrical systems (overhead/underground)

  • Read and interpret construction drawings, work plans, and schematics.

  • Track daily production, material usage, and support logistics and crew coordination

  • Follow and promote strict safety practices, adhering to all OH&S standards

  • Train and support junior workers or apprentices on proper work practices

Qualifications:

  • Journeyman Powerline Technician certification or enrollment/interest in a lineman apprenticeship program

  • 1–3 years of experience in powerline construction (or strong interest with technical training)

  • Ability to climb poles using spikes and rigging and to work from bucket trucks or man-lifts

  • Strong knowledge of distribution powerline construction methods, tools, and safety procedures

  • Ability to read and interpret blueprints and construction documents

  • Class 5 driver’s license required: Class 1 or 3 preferred. Able and willing to provide an acceptable five (5) year Driver's Abstract

  • Physically able to work at heights, lift heavy materials, and work in varying weather conditions

  • Strong communication and teamwork skills

Work Conditions:

  • Outdoor work in diverse and sometimes extreme weather

  • Overtime and weekend work may be needed to meet project deadlines. Ability to work long shifts, 10-12 hour days, in all types of weather

  • Working outdoors in various weather conditions

  • Ability to travel extensively across Saskatchewan. Travel to job sites will be required depending on project location

  • This position is considered a safety sensitive position; all new hires will be required to complete a pre-employment/pre-access medical test that includes a drug and alcohol screening, as well as hearing

Additional Information

Ledcor builds and maintains infrastructure in various industries including oil and gas, transportation, and mining. We’re experts in all infrastructure construction and maintenance related projects. Our vast projects include: full-scale contract mining, earthworks, foundations, highways, bridges, roads, waterways and resources, airports, rapid transit, underground utilities, pipelines, independent power projects and site reclamation.

Our workplace culture has been recognized as one of Canada’s Best Diversity Employers, Canada’s Most Admired Corporate Cultures, and a Top 100 Inspiring Workplace in North America.

We are committed to the long-term success and wellbeing of our employees. Employees are provided with a comprehensive health and benefits package which includes extended health and dental, vision care, disability, and life insurance coverage as well as access to mental health and wellness resources through an employee family assistance program.

Employees also receive a competitive retirement package. These programs are administered through the Union, in accordance with the terms and conditions of the applicable collective agreement.

Employment Equity

At Ledcor we believe diversity, equity, and inclusion should be part of everything we do. We are proud to be an equal-opportunity employer. All qualified individuals, regardless of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity, Veteran status or any other identifying characteristic are encouraged to apply.

Our True Blue team consists of individuals from all backgrounds who contribute diverse perspectives and experiences to Ledcor. We are committed to continuing to build on our culture of empowerment, inclusion and belonging.

Adjustments will be provided in all parts of our hiring process. Applicants need to make their needs known in advance by submitting a request via email. For more information about Ledcor’s Inclusion and Diversity initiatives, please visit our I&D page.

title: Maintenance Tech 2 Plant
description:
Overview

Advanced Drainage Systems is a leading manufacturer of stormwater and onsite septic wastewater solutions, committed to sustainability and innovation. With a vast manufacturing and distribution network, we deliver high-quality products across multiple industries while recycling over half a billion pounds of plastic annually. At ADS, we foster a culture where diverse perspectives drive better ideas, ensuring every employee feels valued, heard, and empowered to make an impact.

Responsibilities

We are looking for a Maintenance Technician II to join our team! In this role, you will perform preventive and reactive maintenance duties required for the manufacturing operations of the plant. Your priorities will include executing PM schedules, repairing equipment to maintain maximum production rates, and maintaining an adequate spare parts inventory. If you are passionate about maintenance and sustainability, we would love to hear from you!

Key Responsibilities

  • Troubleshoot and perform repairs on machines and their controls (extruders, corrugators, extrusion die, DC/AC motor & drives, downstream equipment, chillers, etc.).
  • Maintain and build job skills through company training programs.
  • Contact, specify, and order replacement parts & control components from suppliers as needed.
  • Effectively contribute to CMMS performance through timely PM & Repair Work Order completion while ensuring compliance.
  • First responder to troubleshooting machine problems; contact Electrical Engineering if additional support is needed.
  • Support Daily Management directives and initiatives.
  • Help develop and implement Continuous Improvement projects to improve production efficiencies and safety.
  • Work with Production Staff to ensure the safe and reliable operation of plant equipment.

Qualifications

  • High School / Vocational School Diploma or Equivalent.
  • Ability to lift 50 pounds to a height of 6 feet.
  • Ability to stand and be mobile for a minimum of 8 continuous hours.
  • Ability to perform physical labor for extended periods of time in temperature extremes.
  • Ability to handle materials daily using hands, arms, and legs.
  • Full range of body motion including twisting, bending, reaching, squatting, and mounting/dismounting industrial equipment and vehicles.
  • Technician level - Electrical, hydraulics, pneumatic preferred.
  • Basic PLC and Drive training preferred.
  • +2 years Industrial Maintenance, Electrical Systems and Controls preferred.
  • Plastic process knowledge preferred.

Skills & Competencies

  • Experience using Electrical Test equipment: Voltmeter, Ammeter, ohm meter, Megger, etc.
  • Understanding basic machine function and Sequence of Operations of production machinery.
  • Experienced in reading Electrical, pneumatics and mechanical drawings.
  • Knowledge of NEC, UL508A and NFPA 79 codes.
  • Lock Out Tag Out Safety procedures.
  • Arc Flash and PPE Requirements.
  • Strong industrial mechanical, electrical, plumbing and pneumatic skills including troubleshooting.
  • Strong leadership ability with time management and task prioritization skills.
  • Strong in electrical formulas and mechanical math.
  • Knowledge of 3 Phase Industrial wiring and power distribution.
  • AC & DC Drive / Motor PMs, wiring, programming & troubleshooting.
  • Troubleshoot PLC I/O and networking.
  • Motion Training with Servo Drives and Motors.
  • Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to work with and communicate to other plant personnel as well as company resources and vendors.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office, Excel, and Word.
  • Experience in Root Cause Analysis & Problem Solving.

Company Benefits:

Health & Welfare Benefits: Medical and prescription drug plans, telemedicine (with medical plan), High-Deductible Health Plan (HDHP) and Health Savings Account (HSA), dental and vision plans, virtual physical therapy*, an employee assistance program (EAP)*, and voluntary accident, critical illness, and hospital indemnification programs.

Financial Benefits: Retirement 401k program with company match, an employee stock purchase plan (voluntary), financial wellness planning*, paid parental leave*, flexible spending accounts (FSA) for child care and health care, short-term and long-term disability*, basic life insurance, accidental death and dismemberment (AD&D)*, adoption financial reimbursement*, tuition reimbursement*, voluntary life insurance (for employees, spouses, and children), and voluntary identity theft and fraud protection.

*100% paid by ADS.

Why Join ADS?

Growth Opportunities & Global Presence: With a global network of approximately 70 manufacturing plants and 40 distribution centers, ADS offers extensive opportunities for career growth and development in various locations around the world.

Inclusive and Creative Work Culture: ADS fosters a culture of passion, innovation, and continuous improvement, empowering employees to shape industry-leading solutions. We value diverse perspectives, recognizing that they drive better ideas, processes, and performance. Our workplace is built on respect, inclusivity, and shared responsibility, ensuring every team member feels valued and contributes to our collective success.

Innovative & Sustainability Focused: Advanced Drainage Systems is one of the largest plastic recyclers in North America, ensuring over half a billion pounds of plastic is kept out of landfills every year.

Learn More: https://www.adspipe.com/about-us

EEO Statement

ADS supports an inclusive workplace that values diversity of thought, experience, and background. We celebrate the power of our differences to create better solutions for our customers by ensuring employees can be their authentic selves each day. ADS is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, ancestry, age, physical or mental disability, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, pregnancy, veteran status, marital status, creed, status with regard to public assistance, genetic status or any other status protected by federal, state or local law.

title: Bilingual Recruitment Consultant
description:
Position Type:

Permanent

If you’re looking for a fulfilling career that can make a real difference in your life, and the lives of others, you’ve come to the right place.

As a national health solutions partner, we put people first in everything we do — and that begins with our team of 8,000+ professionals who bring a cross-section of diverse life experiences and career expertise to Medavie. By collaborating and innovating together, our employees are creating industry-leading solutions in insurance, primary care and emergency medical services that impact millions of lives in Canada each year.

Our mission is to improve the wellbeing of Canadians so that every life can be lived to the fullest — and it’s reflected in our award-winning culture. We celebrate individuality and value the diverse perspectives and skills our employees contribute. We go beyond providing competitive pay and comprehensive benefits to offer opportunities for personal and professional growth, flexible work options, meaningful experiences, and supportive leadership. Medavie is where employees can be their best selves, feel they belong, and achieve their full potential. Be part of it by applying for a position with us today.

Are you a passionate recruiter who thrives on connecting top talent with meaningful opportunities? Do you bring energy, creativity, and a strategic mindset to everything you do? If so, we want to meet you!

At Medavie, we’re not just filling roles—we’re shaping the future of healthcare, insurance, and community impact. As a Bilingual Recruitment Consultant, you’ll play a pivotal role in finding and engaging exceptional professionals across healthcare, insurance, customer service, client solutions, and more.

What You’ll Do:

  • Full cycle recruitment - from marketing the position and sourcing to offer acceptance, managing intake discussions, posting, screening, interviewing, and offer delivery, with a focus on delivering an exceptional candidate experience

  • Hiring Leader collaboration – we’re a true extension to business teams, we know the roles, the people and their processes, that’s how we excel in positioning roles to candidates and explaining what their everyday will be like at Medavie

  • Candidate management - reviewing resumes, conducting Bilingual pre-screens and interviews, coordinating next steps, and ensuring timely, transparent communication

  • On-System recruitment management - through our Applicant Tracking System (we use Workday) you’ll ensure accuracy, efficiency, and a seamless experience for candidates and hiring teams

  • Offer execution - you will work with Hiring Leaders to outline competitive offers for candidates and prepare all offer documentation, verbally delivering to candidates and conducting negotiations, guiding candidates and leaders through to acceptance and onboarding

  • Brand ambassador – you will continuously build talent pipelines, engage with passive candidates, and bring innovative marketing strategies to live to attract top talent and stay ahead of trends

  • HR partnership - you will partner with other HR COEs including, HR Business Partners, Learning and Development, Total Compensation, Benefits/Payroll and IT to advance recruitment initiatives, tools, and projects and ensure the candidate experience flows seamlessly

  • Career Events & Talent Engagement - Actively represent Medavie at onsite and virtual career events - engaging talent, promoting career opportunities, strengthening our brand, and driving hiring outcomes

What You Bring:

  • 5+ years of full-cycle recruitment experience (in-house preferred), with a strong background in insurance operations, mid-level roles and unionized hiring

  • Fully bilingual (French/English): French proficiency is required to perform the duties of the role, which include daily collaboration with teams and partners in Québec who operate primarily in French.

title: Diagnostic Sales Specialist - Fort Worth
description:
When presented with an obstacle, do you find another route?

Are you a natural hunter who can seek opportunities?

Are you passionate about medical diagnostics?

If you answered yes to all the above, it sounds like our Diagnostic Specialist career opportunity is the next move for you!

At Hologic we're driven by our purpose, promise and passion to empower people to live healthier lives everywhere, everyday. With ground-breaking technology at the core, our innovations are designed to achieve exceptional clinical results. Making it possible to detect, diagnose and treat illnesses and other health conditions earlier and more effectively.

What does your day to day look like?

  • Effectively communicate and sell the benefits of the APTIMA product line, the ThinPrep Pap Test and adjunctive testing, to clinicians in an assigned geographic territory
  • Develop and execute a sales strategy within your assigned territory to meet and exceed sales goals. Insulate accounts against competition and regain lost customer accounts.
  • Accurately forecast and maintain an individual territory in accordance with a 90-day quota
  • Maintain ongoing business planning with your customers including business reviews with customer financial departments.
  • Maintain consistent and structured communication to District Sales Manager
  • Partner with our marketing department to support the development and execution of marketing programs and sales materials.
  • Attend local and national professional trade shows and events to promote products
  • Update and sync all relevant customer account information into Hologic’s Data Management System daily.

Do you have what it takes?

Education: Bachelor of Arts/Science from an accredited university required

Experience: We want to see your track record (at least 2 years) of delivering commercial success against assigned targets. You will be able to build meaningful relationships with new and existing customers, you will become a trusted partner at solution selling and can influence and negotiate.

Due to the nature of our industry, you will need to understand and articulate complex scientific literature and use complex clinical data as a key factor in your sales process.

Since this position requires extensive driving during the workday, a valid driving license and satisfactory driving record, as well as a serviceable vehicle available for work use is mandatory.

So why join Hologic?

We are committed to making Hologic the company where top talent comes to grow. For you to succeed, we want to enable you with the tools and knowledge required and so we provide comprehensive training when you join as well as continued development and training throughout your career.

We offer a competitive salary and quarterly bonus structure, one of our talent partners can discuss this in more detail with you. From a benefits perspective, you will join our wide-ranging benefits policy including PTO, Employee Stock Purchase Plans, exciting Employee Wellness plans and many more! If you have the right skills and experience and want to join our team, apply today.

We can’t wait to hear from you!

The total compensation range for this role is $140,000 to $145,000. This is based on a base salary and commission plan combination. Final compensation packages will ultimately depend on factors including relevant experience, skillset, knowledge, territory/ geography, education, business needs, market demand and performance versus quota.

Agency and Third Party Recruiter Notice:
Agencies that submit a resume to Hologic must have a current executed Hologic Agency Agreement executed by a member of the Human Resource Department. In addition Agencies may only submit candidates to positions for which they have been invited to do so by a Hologic Recruiter. All resumes must be sent to the Hologic Recruiter under these terms or they will not be considered.

Hologic’s employees are subject to third-party COVID-19 vaccination requirements, including from customers and governmental entities. Hologic is an equal opportunity employer and consistent with federal, state, and local requirements, will consider requests for reasonable accommodation based on disability or sincerely-held religious beliefs where it is able to do so without undue hardship to the company.

Hologic, Inc. is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer inclusive of disability and veterans.

title: Area Leader, Google Cloud Logistics
location: Council Bluffs, IA, USA; Atlanta, GA, USA; Dallas, TX, USA
description:
Product and Business Strategy Leaders bring together teams across Google’s functions to help products execute optimally. Our team pushes Google to scale at key points that refine our products and infrastructure by executing efficiently, bringing solid business sense and sound judgment, and working effectively across organizational lines.

Our roles often include components of strategy (e.g. analyzing and understanding new trends in the industry, building business plans), operations (e.g. running the cadence of organizations, connecting the operating lines between our functions), and communications. Our team partners with senior leadership to run important functions that cross-cut our existing organizations and deliver high impact projects. We help Engineers, PMs, UX, and all of our other functions to build amazing products that delight our users, and then get those products into their hands.

In this role, you will lead a team of logistics professionals responsible for operational execution within warehouse and data center environments. Your role requires extensive cross-functional collaboration with key stakeholders, including Server Operations (the primary internal customer), third-party logistics (3PL) partners, and internal teams managing forecasting, materials, systems, and transportation. As a part of this role, you will require material flow and sequencing knowledge, attention to safety, quality, delivery, cost and people (SQCDP). You will be responsible for delivering regular weekly and monthly updates, conducting formal presentations, and facilitating reviews with executive leadership to report on progress toward strategic objectives and outline corrective actions when necessary.The AI and Infrastructure team is redefining what’s possible. We empower Google customers with breakthrough capabilities and insights by delivering AI and Infrastructure at unparalleled scale, efficiency, reliability and velocity. Our customers include Googlers, Google Cloud customers, and billions of Google users worldwide.

We're behind Google's groundbreaking innovations, empowering the development of AI models, delivering unparalleled computing power to global services, and providing the essential platforms that enable developers to build the future. From software to hardware our teams are shaping the future of world-leading hyperscale computing, with key teams working on the development of our TPUs, Vertex AI for Google Cloud, Google Global Networking, Data Center operations, systems research, and much more.Individual pay is determined by factors including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training.

US: $211000 - $294000 (USD) + 25% bonus target + equity + benefits

Learn more about benefits at Google.
  • Monitor and measure key performance indicators (KPIs) related to Service Level Objective (SLO) achievement, safety adherence, and project/program delivery.
  • Develop and implement a comprehensive forward/reverse logistics strategy that aligns with the organization's long-term goals.
  • Identify opportunities for cost savings, Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) reduction, and capacity optimization through innovative logistics solutions.
  • Evaluate the current organizational effectiveness of the Logistic team and identify areas for improvement.
  • Build and execute a talent development program to enhance the skills and capabilities of team members.

Minimum qualifications:

  • Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Supply Chain operations, or a related technical field, or equivalent practical experience.
  • 13 years of experience in supply chain, operations, or a related field (including third-party logistics (3PL) supplier management, warehouse/fulfillment, automation, and MRP, WMS, or TMS systems), driving transformations, cost reduction, inventory optimization, and working capital improvements.
  • 10 years of experience managing and developing managers.
  • Experience managing supply chain activities (including planning, procurement, production, logistics, and distribution) within manufacturing, 3PL, omnichannel, regulated industries like pharma/biotech.

Preferred qualifications:

  • Master's degree, MBA, or equivalent practical experience.
  • Experience with or active as a Toyota Production System (TPS) practitioner, lean leader, 5S or Gemba advocate, as well as digital tools, AI, advanced analytics and shop floor automation.
  • Experience leading Sales and Operations Planning (S&OP), Integrated Business Planning (IBP), or Demand and Operations Planning (D&OP) management.
  • Experience scaling operations and managing third-party logistics (3PL) labor across multiple sites.
  • Proven track record in removing waste from and improving the velocity of material, information and people flow, as well as Safety and Compliance.

title: Java Tech Lead
description:
GENERAL DESCRIPTION

This role is for a Java Technical Lead with a deep understanding of private banking modules, also acting as a senior developer for Asia Satellite applications.

KEY FEATURES AND ACCOUNTABILITIES

  • Lead the implementation of Java based applications across various private banking domain such as Client Pricing, Portfolio Access Management.
  • Application ownership, managing Operational aspects such as technical upgrades, critical production issues, technical improvements etc.
  • Manage a team of developers, coach and guide them. Interact with software vendors.
  • Passionate about technologies, building robust and scalable interfaces and local developments
  • Hands on experience in analysis, design, coding, and implementation of complex and custom-built solution
  • Work collaboratively with team to achieve goals. Experience working in a Safe Agile environment
  • Drive and review technical and architectural design, enforce architectural guidelines and challenge status quo
  • Stakeholder management – working closely with program stakeholders, business change engineers, and project managers to drive and manage IT delivery. Single point of contact for Product owners and business users.

Regulatory Responsibilities &/OR Risk Management

  • Demonstration of appropriate values and behaviours including but not limited to standards on honesty and integrity, due care and diligence, fair dealing (treating customers fairly), management of conflicts of interest, competence and continuous development, adequate risk management, and compliance with applicable laws and regulations

SKILLS REQUIREMENTS
Professional

  • Minimum 15 years of development experience in Front-end applications, programming languages and back-end queries.
  • Well organized and ability to prioritise and carry out multiple tasks. Able to influence and drive projects to meet key milestones and overcome challenges.
  • Minimum 3 years of a team lead experience
  • Able to translate functional requirements to efficient and fit-for-purpose technical solutions
  • Preferably, have experience in finance/ banking / Wealth management domain
  • Communicate highly technical issues to non-technical audience
  • Understand business / system requirements and translate them into technical solutions that are scalable, stable, and fit for purpose.
  • Ensure adherence to the bank standards (including code, security and performance)
  • Work collaboratively with team to achieve goals. Experience working in Agile methodologies, such as Scrum, and/or Extreme programming

Technical

  • Strong knowledge on Java, JBOSS and Spring boot, Hibernate frameworks.
  • Strong knowledge on Service oriented architecture, RESTful services
  • Strong computer science knowledge (algorithms, data structures, object-oriented design)
  • Relevant higher educational and professional background with ideally 9 years or more of extensive experience in software development including as a backend developer
  • Working knowledge of Kubernetes and containerization, streaming using Kafka, Avro schema and React JS
  • Good knowledge of SQL (MySQL, Oracle, Postgres etc.)
  • Working knowledge of GITLAB repositories, Maven, Octopus, CI / CD pipeline
  • Exposure to cloud platforms would be an advantage
  • Basic Linux shell scripting commands
  • Familiarity with Intellij, SonarQube/SonarLi

Personal and Social

  • Exceptional communication skills to interact with both technical teams and business stakeholders.
  • Detail-oriented mindset with the ability to manage multiples tasks and prioritize effectively
  • Proactive, collaborative, and customer-focused approach to solving problems and driving adoption
  • Mature professional, building & managing a team, presentations to senior management

RANK APPLICABLE TO THE POSITION

  • Rank: Associate Director

title: Associate Territory Manager (SML)
description:
Job Title
Senior Business Development Executive/Associate Territory Manager Function
Commercial Reports To
Branch Manager/Senior Branch Manager Location
Region Travel Required
25% . 50% . 75% .
About DHL Blue Dart Express India:
Blue Dart Express Limited, a listed company, is a leader in courier, integrated air express package distribution, and eCommerce in South Asia, offering a wide range of logistics solutions. Founded in 1983, it is recognised for reliable, time-bound, and secure delivery services across 55,000+ locations in India as well as internationally. Its competencies span across evaluation and contracting, planning and transition, delivery and execution, and specific service capabilities. We pride ourselves on innovation, digitalization, service excellence, and creating long-term partnerships with our clients, enabling businesses to thrive in the fast-paced logistics environment and ever-evolving supply-chains. It wholly-owns India’s only scheduled air cargo airline, Blue Dart Aviation Limited, since May 1994, and the Concorde Air Logistics Limited, a transport, storage, and communications business, since May 2004.
Blue Dart Express Limited is a part of the DHL Group's DHL eCommerce division, leveraging this global logistics leader’s network across 220 countries and territories to provide comprehensive express and logistics solutions to our clients. Our robust network and unmatched infrastructure have positioned the Blue Dart brand as a key India-grown logistics player with an unmatched and phenomenal global outreach rooted in the DHL brand of its majority shareholder.
We are proud to be consistently ranked among India’s Top 100 Great Places to Work, combining it with DHL Group’s Top Employer certification. Being an Employer of Choice is not just a goal; it’s a fundamental part of who we are, committed to creating a workplace where people are valued, empowered, and wear the sobriquet “Blue Darter” with honour.
If you are looking to join a purpose-led, people-first, company that shapes India’s supply-chains with integrity and safety, Blue Dart is where you belong. Blue Dart offers competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits, access to cross-functional, succession planning and global career paths, and strong culture of recognition. Position Overview:
The Senior Business Development Executive/Associate Territory Manager is responsible for driving revenue growth within the assigned Area by effectively managing the sales process for all products, including Domestic Priority (DP), International, Retail, Cargo, and E-Retail. The role will also focus on revenue enhancement through channel partners such as Regional Service Providers (RSPs), Consolidators, Franchise Collection Centres (FCCs), and One-Stop Centres (OSCs). The incumbent will play a crucial role in customer acquisition, relationship management, and adherence to company sales policies. Job Purpose:
This role will focus on managing the end-to-end sales cycle, ensuring customer satisfaction, and driving sustained business development. It requires a highly motivated and target-driven individual with a strong understanding of the logistics and courier industry.
Key Roles & Responsibilities:
Sales & Revenue Growth
• Monitor and drive Area revenue performance to achieve targeted sales growth and profitability.
• Evaluate the profitability of key accounts and take corrective measures to achieve profit targets.
• Track product-wise yields regularly and develop action plans to meet set targets.
• Manage the end-to-end sales process for the Area, ensuring revenue growth for all products.
Channel Partner & Customer Management
• Develop and maintain strong relationships with existing customers, ensuring revenue retention.
• Identify and drive new business opportunities through channel partners.
• Monitor and enhance channel partner performance in terms of revenue, sales, and profitability.
• Support sales capability-building initiatives for sales teams and channel partners.
Sales Operations & Compliance
• Ensure adherence to Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) among sales teams and channel partners.
• Implement sales and marketing strategies to enhance market share and profitability.
• Follow up and ensure closure of product-specific sales leads received from telemarketing or other departments.
• Negotiate rates and service offerings with customers within set approval limits.
• Maintain and update prospect details in the Saffire system on a daily basis.
• Address and resolve customer service issues in coordination with internal and external stakeholders.
• Provide insights to the Branch Sales Head regarding modifications to existing product offerings to enhance revenue and profitability.
Collection & Remittance
• Ensure achievement of collection targets within the set remittance cycle and Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) benchmarks.
People Management
• Guide and support the sales team to ensure high performance and goal alignment.
• Ensure optimal staffing levels within the Area sales team.
Qualifications & Experience:
Education:
• Essential: Graduate degree in Business Administration, Sales, Marketing, or a related field.
• Preferred: An MBA or Postgraduate degree in Sales & Marketing.
Experience:
• 1-3 years of experience in sales, business development, or key account management in the logistics, courier, supply chain, or e-commerce industry.
• Experience in B2B sales, managing channel partners, and driving revenue growth is an added advantage.
Skills & Capabilities:
Technical Skills:
• Strong understanding of sales processes, revenue management, and market expansion strategies.
• Experience in logistics, courier services, or supply chain management.
• Proficiency in using sales tracking tools like Saffire and Avature.
• Knowledge of data analytics and business intelligence tools for tracking sales trends and performance.
Key Competencies:
• Result-Oriented: Strong drive to achieve sales targets and revenue growth.
• Customer-Centric: Ability to develop and maintain strong customer relationships.
• Analytical Thinking: Proactive approach to market analysis and business development.
• Negotiation & Influence: Strong persuasion skills to drive sales and revenue enhancement.
Key Performance Indicators:
KRAs
KPIs
Growth in Area Revenues
% achievement of product-wise and channel-wise revenue targets
Achievement of yield targets (Yield per piece) for all products
Drive Market Growth
% increase in revenues from key industry segments (e.g., Automotive, Life Sciences, etc.) and key accounts
Enhance Revenues via Channel Partners
Revenue targets achieved through RSPs and other channel partners
Ensure Timely Collections
Achievement of Logic Remittance targets
Reduction in outstanding receivables (e.g., 60-day, 90-day, 150-day buckets)
Drive Sales Capability, Productivity, and Process Adherence
Achievement of Sales KPIs and compliance with SOPs
New Product Development
Successful implementation of new products in the Area
Foster a Performance-Driven Culture
Timely adherence to Performance Management System guidelines
Drive Employee Morale and Engagement
Employee retention and engagement metrics
Why join DHL Blue Dart?
When you join us, you’re not just taking a job—you’re becoming part of a purpose-led movement that’s reshaping the future of logistics across India and the world. At Blue Dart, we don’t just deliver parcels—we deliver excellence, innovation, and opportunity every day.
Here’s what makes us stand out:
•
Great Place to Work® Certified—a testament to our people-first culture, inclusive environment, and commitment to your growth and well-being.
•
Competitive pay and performance-linked rewards that truly recognize your impact.
•
Structured learning, global exposure, and on-the-job coaching to help you grow faster than ever.
•
Best-in-class benefits—from comprehensive medical cover (including optional parental care) to financial wellness and generous time off.
•
A culture that celebrates diversity, innovation, and bold thinking—where your voice is heard and your contributions matter.
•
Global career paths across the DHL Group, opening doors to opportunities in 220+ countries and territories.
•
The pride of working with India’s most awarded logistics brand, trusted by millions and driven by purpose.
This is your chance to do meaningful work, build a career without borders, and thrive in a company that’s not just a logistics leader—but a Great Place to Work. Come grow with us.

title: Executive Operations  IAH
description:
Job Title

Executive – Hub Operations

Function
Hub Operations

Reports To
Head – Hub Operations

Location
Air Hub / Surface Hub

Travel Required
25% ☐ 50% ☐ 75% ☐

About DHL Blue Dart Express India:

(Use the same standard About Blue Dart section exactly as in your master JD.)

Position Overview:

The Senior Executive / Assistant Manager – Hub Operations is responsible for managing end-to-end mid-mile operations at an assigned Air Hub or Surface Hub, ensuring seamless movement of shipments across the Blue Dart network. The role oversees inbound, outbound and transit operations, enabling timely network connectivity, operational accuracy and service reliability while maintaining the highest standards of safety, compliance and productivity.

Reporting to the Head – Hub Operations, the incumbent leads shift operations across the hub by coordinating shipment processing, fleet movements, manpower deployment and operational resources. The role works closely with Network Operations, Aviation, Surface Linehaul, Customer Service and Regional Operations teams to ensure network schedules are met, operational exceptions are proactively managed and hub performance supports Blue Dart's service commitments.

Job Purpose:

To manage mid-mile hub operations by ensuring timely movement of shipments across the network, maximising hub productivity, maintaining network connectivity, driving operational excellence and ensuring compliance with organisational standards, safety requirements and service commitments.

Key Roles & Responsibilities:

Hub Operations Management

  • Lead day-to-day operations across the assigned Air Hub or Surface Hub, ensuring efficient inbound, outbound and transit shipment movement.
  • Ensure adherence to network schedules, operational workflows and Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).
  • Plan daily hub activities based on shipment volumes, network priorities and operational requirements.
  • Ensure optimum utilisation of hub infrastructure, operational resources and manpower.
  • Support the Head – Hub Operations in achieving operational, service quality and productivity objectives.

Mid Mile Network Operations

  • Ensure timely receipt, processing, consolidation and dispatch of shipments across the Blue Dart network.
  • Coordinate movement of shipments through scheduled air, surface and feeder networks to maintain network connectivity.
  • Monitor loading, unloading, scanning, tallying and dispatch activities to ensure shipment accuracy.
  • Coordinate vehicle placement and dock activities to minimise turnaround times.
  • Ensure timely movement of priority, premium and time-definite shipments across the network.

Network Coordination & Exception Management

  • Monitor adherence to network schedules and proactively manage delays, misconnections, misroutes and operational exceptions.
  • Coordinate with Aviation, Surface Linehaul, Network Operations and Regional Operations teams to resolve operational issues.
  • Ensure timely communication of network disruptions and recovery actions to impacted stakeholders.
  • Monitor stock lying at hub (SLAH) and drive timely clearance of pending shipments.
  • Ensure accurate status updates and operational visibility across all network systems.

Workforce & Shift Leadership

  • Lead operational employees, supervisors and outsourced workforce during assigned shifts.
  • Plan manpower deployment based on shipment volumes and operational priorities.
  • Conduct daily shift briefings and communicate operational updates and safety requirements.
  • Monitor workforce productivity, attendance and adherence to operational standards.
  • Support capability building through coaching, training and performance feedback.

Fleet, Capacity & Resource Management

  • Ensure availability and optimum utilisation of vehicles, trailers and operational equipment to support scheduled network movements.
  • Coordinate with fleet partners and transport vendors to minimise delays and maximise vehicle utilisation.
  • Monitor dock utilisation, bay allocation and turnaround times to improve hub efficiency.
  • Support capacity planning during peak volumes, network changes and seasonal demand fluctuations.
  • Ensure operational readiness of manpower, equipment and infrastructure to meet business requirements.

Service Quality & Operational Excellence

  • Drive adherence to network timelines and ensure timely connectivity of shipments across the air or surface network.
  • Monitor shipment accuracy, scanning compliance, loading quality and dispatch integrity to minimise operational errors.
  • Ensure timely closure of operational exceptions, stock lying at hub (SLAH), misroutes and misconnections.
  • Review operational performance trends and implement corrective actions to improve service reliability.
  • Drive continuous improvement initiatives to enhance hub productivity, service quality and operational efficiency.

Safety, Security & Compliance

  • Ensure compliance with organisational policies, operational SOPs, aviation or transportation regulations and statutory requirements.
  • Promote a strong safety culture by ensuring safe handling of shipments, vehicles and operational equipment.
  • Support periodic operational, security and quality audits and ensure timely closure of audit observations.
  • Ensure compliance with all regulatory documentation, shipment security requirements and operational controls applicable to the hub.
  • Identify operational risks and implement preventive measures to ensure uninterrupted hub operations.

Stakeholder Management & Operational Communication

  • Coordinate closely with Network Operations, Aviation, Surface Operations, Customer Service, Security and Regional Operations teams to ensure seamless execution.
  • Maintain effective communication with upstream and downstream network locations regarding shipment movements, operational disruptions and recovery plans.
  • Ensure timely escalation of operational risks, capacity constraints and service issues to the Head – Hub Operations.
  • Build collaborative relationships with internal stakeholders and transport partners to improve network performance.
  • Support implementation of organisational initiatives, process changes and operational improvement programmes across the hub.

People Leadership & Capability Development

  • Lead, coach and develop supervisors, operational employees and outsourced workforce to achieve operational objectives.
  • Conduct regular shift briefings and ensure effective communication of operational priorities, safety requirements and service expectations.
  • Monitor employee productivity, attendance, discipline and overall team performance.
  • Support implementation of learning initiatives and on-the-job capability development programmes.
  • Foster a culture of accountability, teamwork, continuous improvement and customer focus across the hub.

Qualifications & Experience:

Education

  • Bachelor's Degree in any discipline.
  • Degree or certification in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Operations Management or Transportation Management will be an added advantage.

Experience

  • Senior Executive – Hub Operations (Grade N): 3–5 years of experience in hub operations, mid-mile logistics, transportation or express distribution.
  • Assistant Manager – Hub Operations (Grade M): 4 - 7 years of relevant experience with demonstrated experience in leading medium to large hub operations.
  • Experience in managing inbound, outbound and transit operations within an air or surface logistics network.
  • Exposure to fleet coordination, shipment movement, operational planning and network performance management.
  • Experience in leading frontline operational teams and working in high-volume, time-sensitive logistics environments

Skills & Capabilities:

Technical Skills

  • Hub & Mid Mile Operations Management
  • Air & Surface Network Operations
  • Shipment Processing & Transit Management
  • Fleet Coordination & Capacity Utilisation
  • Network Planning & Schedule Adherence
  • Service Quality & Operational Excellence
  • Operational MIS & Performance Analytics
  • Safety, Security & Regulatory Compliance
  • Process Improvement & Productivity Enhancement
  • Microsoft Office & Logistics Management Systems

Behavioural Competencies

  • Operational Leadership
  • Decision Making Under Pressure
  • Planning & Organising
  • Analytical & Problem-Solving Skills
  • Team Leadership & People Development
  • Cross-Functional Collaboration
  • Customer Focus
  • Accountability & Ownership
  • Adaptability & Resilience
  • Continuous Improvement Mindset

Key Performance Indicators:

Sr. No.

Key Result Areas

Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)

1

Network Performance

On-time departure & arrival adherence, Network connectivity, Schedule adherence

2

Service Quality

SLAH %, Misroutes, Misconnections, Shipment processing accuracy, Scan compliance

3

Hub Productivity

Hub throughput, Vehicle turnaround time, Dock utilisation, Productivity per FTE

4

Fleet & Capacity Utilisation

Fleet utilisation, Vehicle availability, Capacity utilisation, Dispatch efficiency

5

Operational Excellence

Exception closure within TAT, Operational audit compliance, SOP adherence

6

Safety & Compliance

Safety compliance, Security compliance, Regulatory adherence, Audit scores

7

People Management

Team productivity, Employee engagement, Training completion, Attrition management

8

Continuous Improvement

Process improvement initiatives, Cost optimisation, Productivity enhancement, Operational efficiency gains

Why join DHL Blue Dart?

When you join us, you're not just taking a job—you’re becoming part of a purpose-led movement that's reshaping the future of logistics across India and the world. At Blue Dart, we don't just deliver parcels—we deliver excellence, innovation, and opportunity every day.

Here's what makes us stand out:

  • Great Place to Work® Certified—a testament to our people-first culture, inclusive environment, and commitment to your growth and well-being.
  • Competitive pay and performance-linked rewards that truly recognize your impact.
  • Structured learning, global exposure, and on-the-job coaching to help you grow faster than ever.
  • Best-in-class benefits—from comprehensive medical cover (including optional parental care) to financial wellness and generous time off.
  • A culture that celebrates diversity, innovation, and bold thinking—where your voice is heard and your contributions matter.
  • Global career paths across the DHL Group, opening doors to opportunities in 220+ countries and territories.
  • The pride of working with India's most awarded logistics brand, trusted by millions and driven by purpose.

This is your chance to do meaningful work, build a career without borders, and thrive in a company that's not just a logistics leader—but a Great Place to Work. Come grow with us.

title: Automotive Detail Technician - FULL TIME
description:
Bring the Shine. Build a Career.

Do you take pride in a job well done? Are you someone who notices the little details that make a big difference? If so, we'd love to meet you!

Kocourek Imports is looking for a motivated automotive Detail Technician to join our team. Whether you're experienced or looking to start a new career, we'll provide the training you need to succeed.

As a Detail Technician, you'll help create exceptional first impressions by ensuring every vehicle looks its absolute best before it's delivered to our guests.

What You'll Do

  • Wash, dry, and detail vehicle exteriors.

  • Vacuum and clean vehicle interiors.

  • Clean windows, mirrors, dashboards, door jambs, and upholstery.

  • Apply waxes, protectants, and interior conditioners.

  • Shampoo carpets and upholstery as needed.

  • Inspect vehicles for cleanliness and quality before delivery.

  • Move vehicles safely throughout the dealership.

  • Maintain a clean and organized work area.

  • Follow all safety procedures and company standards.

What We're Looking For

  • Strong attention to detail.

  • Dependable with a positive attitude.

  • Ability to work efficiently in a fast-paced environment.

  • Ability to work independently and as part of a team.

  • Valid driver's license with an acceptable driving record.

  • Ability to stand, bend, lift, and work both indoors and outdoors.

  • Previous detailing experience is a plus—but not required. We'll train the right person!

Why You'll Love Working Here

  • Paid training

  • Competitive pay

  • Full-time, stable employment

  • Opportunities for career growth within the dealership

  • Friendly, team-oriented work environment

  • Employee discounts

  • Comprehensive benefits package including:

    • Medical, Dental & Vision Insurance

    • 401(k) with Company Match

    • Paid Time Off

    • Life Insurance

    • Employee Assistance Program

    • Additional voluntary benefits


QUALIFICATIONS Basic automotive knowledge required. Past experience detailing with knowledge of what the process entails preferred. Experience using a buffer and carpet extractor preferred. Valid driver’s license required in order to perform essential job functions.

HOURS Full time, Monday-Friday. They may be asked or scheduled to work Saturdays.

ATTIRE Kocourek uniforms

PHYSICAL DEMANDS * Periodic lifting and carrying objects over 50 pounds.* Reaching above and below shoulder level.* Able to push materials/parts carts and push cars (with assistance).* Extended periods of kneeling, bending, squatting and stooping to detail vehicles.* Manual dexterity and eye/hand coordination to operate equipment.* May be exposed to repetitive tasks involving hand and arm motion.

* May be required to inspect vehicles outdoors in inclement weather.* May be exposed to fumes, chemicals, high levels of dust and noise in repair center.

WORK ENVIRONMENT Service Department/Detail Shop

Kocourek Automotive is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate in hiring or employment on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, or any other basis prohibited by federal, state, or local law.

description:
